title: Satureja × perez-pazii
taxonomy: Satureja × perez-pazii is classified within the family Lamiaceae, which includes other popular herbs such as mint, basil, and rosemary. This plant is a hybrid species, specifically a cross between two species of the genus Satureja. Satureja × perez-pazii is native to the Iberian Peninsula, where it is found growing in dry areas and rocky outcrops. The classification of this plant reflects its close relationship with other members of the Satureja genus. Its classification within the Lamiaceae family highlights its characteristics as a perennial herb with small, fragrant flowers.

intro: Satureja × perez-pazii, a hybrid of the Satureja genus, is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its distinctive aroma and flavor. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues and respiratory problems.
